aliases: Prawn Burger with Spicy Tomato Sauce

english: Prawn Burger with Spicy Tomato Sauce

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002473 covers hamburger as a bread-bun sandwich with a protein patty. The Spicy Tomato Ebi Burger uses a prawn (ebi) patty with spicy tomato sauce rather than a beef patty; this is the closest defensible FoodOn anchor. No exact FoodOn code exists for a prawn burger variant.
ontology.foodex2_description: Burger (sandwich with meat/fish patty in bun)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers burgers broadly including fish/seafood patty variants. Used as the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor for this prawn-patty burger.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for a prawn burger fast-food product. SNOMED CT food coding is limited for composite fast-food items.

serving.natural_language_portion: One Spicy Tomato Ebi Burger weighs approximately 160 g, comprising the prawn patty, burger bun, spicy tomato sauce, and condiments as served.

nutrient_source.title: Spicy Tomato Ebi Burger — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Spicy Tomato Ebi Burger (cr_id G00010105). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken verbatim from the HPB record. Saturated fat is not reported in the HPB entry and is set to -1. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and other micronutrients not reported by HPB are set to -1.

gi_source.title: Hamburger bun / white bread burger — analogous GI estimate based on refined wheat bread products
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Spicy Tomato Ebi Burger. GI estimated at 70 (high) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for white hamburger bun / refined wheat bread products (GI range 70–75). The burger bun is the dominant carbohydrate contributor (29.57g/100g carbohydrate, 1.8g/100g fibre). The prawn patty and sauce contribute negligible additional carbohydrate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (70 × 47.3) / 100 ≈ 33.1, based on 160g serving providing approximately 47.3g carbohydrate.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (487 mg/100g) with low potassium offset (197 mg/100g); sodium-to-potassium ratio is unfavourable at approximately 2.5:1
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this burger due to its high sodium load of 487 mg/100g (approximately 779 mg per 160g serving), which represents over one-third of the 2000 mg daily sodium target; requesting reduced spicy tomato sauce and no mayonnaise can lower sodium intake marginally.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI refined wheat bun (GI ~70) with 29.57g/100g carbohydrate and only 1.8g/100g dietary fibre, producing a high glycaemic load of approximately 33 per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this burger or consume it only occasionally in small portions, as the refined-flour bun delivers a high glycaemic load (~33 per serving) that will cause rapid postprandial glucose elevation; substituting a wholemeal bun and reducing sauce quantity would partially mitigate the glycaemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Deep-fried prawn patty contributes cholesterol (29 mg/100g) and likely saturated and trans fats from commercial frying oils; high sodium further elevates cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this burger due to the combined cardiovascular risk from high sodium (487 mg/100g), cholesterol from the prawn patty (29 mg/100g), and likely saturated and trans fats introduced by deep-frying in commercial oils; the spicy tomato sauce and mayonnaise add further sodium and omega-6-rich fat burden.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-fried preparation generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that promote joint inflammation; tomato sauce contains nightshade solanine; commercial frying oils may contain trans fats
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this burger as the deep-fried prawn patty generates pro-inflammatory AGEs, the spicy tomato sauce introduces nightshade solanine which may exacerbate symptoms in sensitive individuals, and commercial frying oils likely contribute trans fats that are strongly pro-inflammatory.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Spicy Tomato Ebi Burger is a Singapore fast-food burger featuring a deep-fried prawn (ebi) patty served in a refined wheat flour burger bun, topped with a tangy and mildly spicy tomato sauce, mayonnaise, and lettuce. 'Ebi' is the Japanese word for prawn, reflecting the Japanese-influenced fast-food culture prevalent in Singapore's quick-service restaurant sector. The prawn patty is typically breaded and deep-fried, yielding a crispy exterior with a tender, juicy prawn interior. The spicy tomato sauce provides a tangy, slightly sweet, and mildly spicy flavour profile that complements the savoury prawn patty. Per 100g, the burger provides 263 kcal, 13.3g protein, 10.18g fat, 29.57g carbohydrate, 5.95g sugar, 1.8g dietary fibre, and 487mg sodium. The high sodium content (487 mg/100g) and high-GI refined bun (GI ~70) make this burger unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The deep-fried preparation generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs), which are pro-inflammatory and of concern for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis. The burger is a popular lunch and dinner option in Singapore and is classified as an ultra-processed bread-based dish in the grains and staples food group. Calcium content is notably high at 116 mg/100g, likely contributed by the bun and prawn patty. Iron content is 2.87 mg/100g. This item is suitable for halal diets but contains gluten (wheat bun), crustaceans (prawn), and egg (mayonnaise) as major allergens.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010105, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved source for Singapore food records. The food identity is exact. GI is estimated from the closest analogous approved source (University of Sydney GI Database, white hamburger bun) and clearly documented as an analogous estimate.
notes: 1. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these fields are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this item (G00010105) as of April 2026. No supplementary approved source was available to fill these gaps without risk of misattribution. 2. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate based on white hamburger bun data from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Spicy Tomato Ebi Burger exists in any approved source. 3. Trans fat is noted in the input health_suitability section as approximately 0.1g/100g from commercial frying oils; however, this value is not confirmed in the HPB source and is therefore set to -1 pending verification. 4. Halal status is assumed based on typical Singapore fast-food prawn burger formulations; consumers should verify with the specific restaurant operator. 5. All core macros (protein_g 13.3, fat_g 10.18, carbohydrate_g 29.57) are non-zero and sourced from HPB; no zero-macro justification is required.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 2: Corrected ontology match types from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent'. Ensured 'Là fānqié xiā bǎo' present in flat aliases array.

Iteration 3: Identified and corrected Unicode script mixing in Tamil alias entry. Previous draft had 'காரமான தक्काளி இறால் பர்கர்' with mixed Tamil/Devanagari characters in aliases_by_language, causing verbatim mismatch against the flat aliases array entry. Corrected to pure Tamil script 'காரமான தக்காளி இறால் பர்கர்' consistently in both aliases_by_language.tamil and flat aliases array. Full aliases consistency self-check re-run and confirmed passing.
